content.rendered:
1966: The Castiles play a gig at the Hullabaloo in Middletown, New Jersey.

1971: The Bruce Springsteen Band is in residence at the Student Prince in Asbury Park.

1974: Bruce drops by WMMR-FM in Philadelphia to record a very special Christmas message for DJ Ed Sciaky.

1976: Bruce joins Patti Smith on stage for both of her shows at the Bottom Line in New York City, playing guitar and piano for a remarkable twelve songs between the two shows (although a couple of the songs may be more accurately described as improv pieces).

1984: Bruce plays a Born in the U.S.A. show in Dallas, but the set list has almost as many songs from Darkness on the Edge of Town, including the tour premiere of “Factory.”

1988: Bruce joins Southside Johnny and The Asbury Jukes on stage at The Stone in San Francisco for four songs, including Bruce’s first-ever performance of “Hearts of Stone.” His second performance won’t happen for a quarter-century.

1995: Bruce plays a solo acoustic show at the Wiltern Theater in Los Angeles, where he tour premieres “Nebraska” and “Dead Man Walkin’.”

1996: Bruce wraps up his three-night acoustic stand at the Paramount Theater in Asbury Park, once again joined by a posse of friends. Tonight’s guest include Danny, Soozie, Patti, Steve, Vini, Big Danny Gallagher, and Richard Blackwell. For a solo show, Bruce is seldom on stage by himself. The setlist features the tour premieres of “All That Heaven Will Allow” and “I Don’t Want to Go Home.” This show is available as part of Bruce’s official archive series.

2007: The Magic Tour moves to its second European stop, the Bizkaia Arena in Bilbao.

2009: Bruce appears with Bono and Mick Jagger on the cover of Rolling Stone.

2012: After a week off for Thanksgiving, the Wrecking Ball Tour resumes in Vancouver. The set list features no premieres, but a couple of songs appear in surprising positions: Bruce opens with “Shackled and Drawn,” and “Racing in the Street” makes an unusual encore appearance.

2017: Springsteen on Broadway continues its theatrical run at the Walter Kerr Theater on Broadway. After tonight’s show, Bruce will take more than a week off.

2024: Bruce drops by the set of Deliver Me From Nowhere, filming today in Bayonne, New Jersey.

excerpt.rendered: On this date: Bruce makes a guest appearance with Patti Smith, debuts “Hearts of Stone,” and records a Christmas greeting for Ed Sciaky. Plus: a very crowded “solo” stage in Asbury Park and much more.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Kingdom of Days: December 30
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: “Streets of Philadelphia," "Because the Night" and Jessica Rae Springsteen all debuted on this date. Video and more highlights inside.

jetpack-related-posts.title: November 24
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: This date in Springsteen history gave us some gems: check out the unreleased 1980 concert video from Largo, listen to highlights from Bruce's trio of Asbury Park concerts in 1996, and more.

jetpack-related-posts.title: November 3
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: On this date: Bruce gives a revealing radio interview, plays "Prove It '78" for the last time for 32 years, and makes the first of many Light of Day appearances. More highlights and tons of video inside.

jetpack-related-posts.title: September 17
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: On this date: Born to Run hits #1, Bruce debuts his duet with Sting on "Every Breath You Take," and a tantalizing audio glimpse of a still-unreleased, never-performed original song.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Kingdom of Days: December 3
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Today in Springsteen history: Bruce releases two new videos, a star-studded holiday show in Asbury Park features the return of some notable set list prodigals, and an epic 20-minute "Kitty's Back." More highlights inside.

jetpack-related-posts.title: October 16
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: On this date: Bruce debuts "Long Time Comin'" nine years before its album release, a star-studded show at the Roxy, Bruce and Billy Joel perform for Barack Obama, and more.
